type.en: Reason why substance was added to the report
type.fr: Raison pour laquelle la substance a été ajoutée au rapport

comments.comment: With the phase out of coal, the increased use of natural gas was observed in 2019.  Therefore emission by-products from coal combustion (ie. hydrochlorobenzene, dioxins and furans) are no longer expected from the site.  As a result, natural gas is used as the main fuel at the facility.  Therefore this results in increased emissions of NOx and CO.

type.en: Reason why substance was added to the report
type.fr: Raison pour laquelle la substance a été ajoutée au rapport

comments.comment: With the phase out of coal, the increased use of natural gas was observed in 2019.  Therefore emission by-products from coal combustion (ie. hydrochlorobenzene, dioxins and furans) are no longer expected from the site.  As a result, natural gas is used as the main fuel at the facility.  Therefore this results in increased emissions of NOx and CO.

comments.comment: Brandon Generating Station has approval to utilize wet lagoon ash for various applications. We expect to utilize 100 % of the ash, or more, each year.

type.fr: Commentaire sur les rejets sur le site

comments.comment: Phosphorus in coal ash is released to a wet lagoon for storage. Decant water from the lagoon is discharged to the Assiniboine River. We estimate that 0.186 tonnes of phosphorus was released to the Assiniboine River in 2000.
